task_completion.description: GIMP is open with an image loaded in the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible, displaying an image (either a photograph or a synthetic image with geometric shapes) in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The active foreground color is set to bright red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The foreground color swatch in the GIMP toolbox or color selection dialog shows a bright pure red color (RGB 255,0,0 or #FF0000).

task_completion.description: The Bucket Fill tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bucket Fill tool icon (a tipping paint bucket) is highlighted/active in the GIMP toolbox, or the tool options dock shows settings specifically for the Bucket Fill tool.

task_completion.description: A bounded area in the image is flood-filled with bright red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct, connected region within the image canvas has been colored bright red. The red area should be clearly visible and substantial (not just a few pixels).

task_completion.description: The red fill is confined to a specific bounded region and does not cover the entire image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The red color respects the boundaries of a shape or region in the image. Less than half of the total image is red, proving the agent did not just paint the entire canvas red.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus, toolbox, and canvas, rather than terminal commands executing ImageMagick or Python scripts to alter the image.

integrity.description: The fill appears to be a genuine bucket fill rather than a painted overlay.
integrity.visual_evidence: The red area perfectly conforms to the existing boundaries, lines, or color regions of the original image, rather than looking like a manually drawn rectangle or messy brush stroke.

integrity.description: The image was modified during the trajectory, not pre-loaded with the answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Early screenshots show the original image without the red fill, and later screenshots show the red fill applied.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP's Bucket Fill tool (shortcut Shift+B) to fill a bounded area with pure red (RGB 255,0,0 or #FF0000). A successful fill must be a connected region of at least 500 pixels, have a bright red color (R>=180), and represent a meaningful modification (less than 50% of the total image changed, meaning the entire image shouldn't just be painted red). The image will either be a complex photograph or a synthetic image with geometric shapes.
